meta.title: Gen SEA Summit 2026 — Apply
meta.description: Three days in Khon Kaen. 350+ founders, investors, and ministers from 10+ countries. Apply as a delegate or submit a venture for Gen SEA Summit 2026.

applyPicker.title: Choose your application path
applyPicker.subtitle: Two ways to join Gen SEA Summit 2026 — pick the one that fits you.

delegate.title: Apply as Delegate
delegate.body: For students and recent graduates (18–30) seeking scholarships up to 100% of the USD 1,800 program value.

venture.title: Submit a Venture
venture.body: For early-stage founders building in ASEAN. Selected ventures join the Gen SEA Ventures 33 cohort with a USD 9,000+ package.

hero.imageAlt: Golden hour view of a Thai temple skyline with a modern city behind it
hero.subhead: Three days. 350+ founders, investors, and ministers from 10+ countries. Apply as an individual or as a startup — both tracks open below.

validation.phone: Please enter a valid phone number
validation.foundedYearMax: Year cannot be in the future

header.title: Apply for Gen SEA Summit 2026
header.subtitle: Five steps. About 25 minutes. You can come back to finish later — answers stay in this browser.

errors.invalidPhone: Please enter a valid phone number
errors.contactRequired: Provide LINE or Discord (at least one)
errors.essayMin: Essay must be at least 200 words
errors.essayMax: Essay must be at most 500 words
errors.pdfOnly: Pitch deck must be a PDF
errors.videoTooLarge: Video file must be 100MB or smaller
errors.eligibility: At least one founder must have graduated within the last 5 years
errors.instEmail: Use an institutional email (.ac.th or partner university domain)

identity.heading: Who are you?
identity.subhead: Use your real name as it appears on your student ID. We'll use this on your fellowship badge.

fields.fullNameThPlaceholder: เช่น สมชาย ใจดี
fields.fullNameThHelp: Optional if you do not write Thai.
fields.emailHelp: We send updates here. Use your university address if you have one.
fields.contactGroupHelp: We need at least one of LINE or Discord to coordinate during the program.

academic.heading: Where are you studying?
academic.subhead: Pick your university first — the faculty list updates to match. If your university isn't listed, choose Other.

fields.universitySearchEmpty: No matches. Try a different spelling or pick Other.
fields.gpaHelp: Out of 4.0. Leave blank if your institution uses a different scale.

recommender.heading: Your recommender
recommender.subhead: One academic reference. We will not contact them without your prompting — the letter you upload is what we read.

fields.name: Recommender's full name
fields.title: Title
fields.emailHelp: Must end in .ac.th or match a partner university domain.

essay.heading: Your essay
essay.subhead: 200–500 words. Two prompts in one — we want to read both threads inside the same answer.
promptBullets: Why you want to be in this room. Specifically: who do you want to be in conversation with?
A venture, project, or sector question you are already working on (or can't stop thinking about).

fields.essayPlaceholder: Start writing. We're reading for clarity over polish.

logistics.heading: Logistics & consent
logistics.subhead: A few practical questions and the consent items required to submit.

accommodation.label: Do you need accommodation?
accommodation.disclosure: Heads up — accommodation is provided only for the Top 25 finalists. Choose Yes to indicate need; we'll confirm coverage on acceptance.

accessibility.label: Accessibility needs
accessibility.placeholder: e.g. step-free venue, sign interpretation, none
accessibility.help: Anything we should know to make the three days work for you.

consents.heading: Consent
consents.pdpa: I consent to Gen SEA Summit processing my personal data under Thailand's PDPA for the purpose of this application. (Required)
consents.photo: I consent to being photographed and filmed during the program for use in Gen SEA Summit's communications. (Optional)
consents.truthful: I confirm that everything I have submitted is true and complete to the best of my knowledge. (Required)

success.title: Application received.
success.subtitle: Your seat in the room is one step closer.
success.body: We've logged your application. Shortlist interviews run June 10–20. Final acceptances drop July 1. Watch your inbox — and your spam folder.
tips: Add team@seabridge.space to your contacts so our emails don't land in spam.
Block out July 16–18 in Khon Kaen, just in case.
Tell one person what you're working on this week. The room rewards momentum.

venture.label: VENTURE
venture.title: Submit your venture for Gen SEA Ventures 33
venture.subtitle: Tell us about your team and what you're building. Selected ventures join the main cohort of Gen SEA Summit 2026.

founder.title: About you
founder.subtitle: The lead founder applying on behalf of the venture

venture.title: About your venture
venture.subtitle: The basics — name, sector, stage, location
venture.name: Venture name
venture.oneLinerPlaceholder: e.g. We help ASEAN smallholder farmers reach fair-trade buyers via mobile
venture.problemPlaceholder: Be specific — who has this problem, why now, and where today's solutions fall short
venture.solutionPlaceholder: How is your approach different? What makes it defensible?

traction.title: Traction & real numbers
traction.subtitle: Real numbers, even if small. Honesty matters more than scale at this stage.
traction.hasLaunched: Have you launched a product or service?
traction.partnershipsPlaceholder: Universities, corporates, government bodies, or other partners
traction.milestonesPlaceholder: Awards, pilots, paying customers, press, regulatory wins — anything that signals momentum

documents.title: Documents & narrative
documents.subtitle: Upload your pitch deck and tell us why Gen SEA Summit matters to you
documents.nominatedByHint: If a partner organisation nominated you, list them here for selection priority
documents.nominatedByPlaceholder: e.g. NIA, TED Fund, NUS Enterprise, BLOCK71
documents.whyGenSeaPlaceholder: What do you hope to walk away with — investors, customers, hires, partnerships, validation? Be specific (200–500 words)

consent.title: Final details & consent
consent.subtitle: Last details before you submit
consent.accommodationHint: Provided only for top-tier ventures. Other selected ventures receive a discounted booking link.
consent.availableAllDays: Will you be available all three days (16–18 July 2026) in Khon Kaen?
consent.interestedInDemoSlot: Interested in a demo slot on the main stage on day one?
consent.demoSlotHint: Standout ventures from the cohort get a live demo slot.
consent.pdpaConsent: I consent to SEA Bridge collecting and processing my personal data and venture details, and to sharing them with Summit sponsors, investors, and partners under Thailand's Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA).
consent.ipShareConsent: I understand the information in this application may be reviewed by selection panellists, judges, and accredited investors during the Summit. Confidential or trade-secret information should not be included.
consent.truthfulnessConfirm: I confirm everything I have submitted is true and accurate, and that I am authorised to submit this application on behalf of my venture.
consent.photoConsent: I consent to Gen SEA Summit using the venture name, logo, and team photos in promotional materials.

success.title: Application received.
success.body: Thank you for sharing your venture with us. The team is reviewing every application closely. Selection results land on 7 June 2026.
success.next1Body: We've sent a confirmation to the email you provided. If it doesn't arrive in 30 minutes, check your spam folder.
success.next2Body: Every applicant hears back on 7 June 2026. Selected ventures receive onboarding details for Gen SEA Bootcamp.
success.next3Body: Follow @seabridge on LinkedIn for cohort announcements, partner updates, and Summit news.

items.label: Hosted in Khon Kaen + Isan Creative Festival

theme.heading: Building Amidst Chaos.
theme.body: Climate, conflict, capital flight, AI displacement — the next ten years will not be patient with anyone who waits for permission. Gen SEA Summit brings together the under-30s already building inside the storm: founders, organisers, researchers, artists, civil servants. Three days. Five sectors. One question — what will you have built before you turn thirty?
theme.pull: We're not looking for the most polished applicants. We're looking for the ones already moving.

sponsor.body: Help us make this happen. We're partnering with organizations who believe in ASEAN's next generation of builders.

individual.heading: Be one of 50.
individual.subhead: For students and recent graduates, ages 18–30, motivated to understand how ventures are built and scaled in ASEAN — whether or not they have a startup of their own.
benefits: Ticket to 3 days at Gen SEA Summit 2026, Khon Kaen
Room with 350+ founders, investors, and ministers from 10+ countries
Gen SEA Bootcamp — USD 1,500 standalone value, included
Scholarships up to 100% of the USD 300 Delegate Pass
Fast-track to SEA Bridge Fellowships and partner internships
Khon Kaen during Isan Creative Festival 2026

steps.body: Apply directly, or be nominated by your university (up to 10 ranked) or faculty (up to 5 ranked).
Top 50 selected on Bootcamp Capstone + application strength.

individual.stepsNote: Nomination prioritizes your application — it does not guarantee selection.

timeline.label: Applications & nominations open. Bootcamp begins immediately.

startup.heading: Join the 33.
startup.subhead: For youth-led early-stage ventures where at least one founder is aged 18–30 and graduated within 5 years.
startup.tag: Building in Wellness, Food, AI, Creative, or Education — across Thailand, ASEAN, or internationally entering ASEAN.
startup.benefitsKicker: What you get · Total package USD 9,000+
benefits: Featured in the Gen SEA Summit 2026 Venture Catalogue (part of ASEAN Youth White Paper distribution)
Venture deep-dive brief — market sizing, ASEAN expansion mapping, partner-matching analysis (USD 6,000 value)
Gen SEA Bootcamp (USD 1,500 standalone value, included)
Accommodation for 1 founder, 2 nights (covered by KKU APDS)
Day 2 closed-door access — TED Fund, NIA, regional VCs, corporate venture arms
Public recognition at Gen SEA Summit Gala, 17 July evening
startup.benefitsFootnote: Notable ventures selected by the organizing committee are offered a live demo slot on the Summit's main stage.

steps.body: Apply directly, or be nominated by your incubation center, science park, or university entrepreneurship program.
Submit your venture overview, sector, and (recommended) a 2-minute video pitch.
33 ventures selected based on founder commitment, problem clarity, ASEAN relevance, and cohort diversity.

startup.stepsNote: For Thai ventures: TED Fund and NIA portfolio ventures are strongly encouraged to apply through the standard pathway.

timeline.label: Open call and institutional nominations open.
Gen SEA Ventures 33 cohort publicly announced.
Final venture brief submitted for Catalogue.
Live demo slots and Gala recognition, Day 1.

sectors.heading: Where the work is happening
sectors.body: Each track is led by practitioners who have shipped at scale across ASEAN. You'll spend the three days deep in one sector and adjacent to the other four.

items.name: Wellness & Public Health
items.blurb: Mental health infrastructure, ageing populations, frontline care that actually scales.

items.name: Food & Agriculture
items.blurb: Climate-resilient farming, supply chains the next generation can inherit, food sovereignty.

items.name: AI & Frontier Tech
items.blurb: Sovereign models, applied research, the tooling layer Southeast Asia keeps having to import.

items.name: Creative Industries
items.blurb: Story, design, music, games — the soft power infrastructure of a region the world is finally watching.

items.name: Education & Talent
items.blurb: What replaces the degree, who teaches the next million engineers, why so many leave for nothing back home.

whoCanApply.heading: Eligibility

criteria.label: Geography
criteria.value: Open to any nationality, with priority for ASEAN

criteria.label: Status
criteria.value: Currently studying, recently graduated, or building independently

whoCanApply.footnote: If you are working on something you would defend in a room full of strangers, you are eligible.

howToGetIn.heading: Three steps. No tricks.

steps.title: Apply
steps.body: Five-step application. Identity, academics, one recommender, a 200–500 word essay, logistics. Plan for 25 minutes.

steps.title: Interview
steps.body: Shortlisted candidates meet a sector lead and an alum on a 20-minute call. We're listening for what you're actually building.

steps.title: Confirm your seat
steps.body: Acceptance lands by July 1. Confirm within 7 days. Travel and accommodation guidance follows for the Top 25.

howToGetIn.caveatBody: If a partner organisation nominated you, your application moves to the front of the queue. It does not skip the interview. Every accepted student earns the seat the same way.

items.label: Gen SEA Summit in Khon Kaen

finalCta.heading: The room is small. The window is open.
finalCta.body: Delegate applications close 15 May. Venture applications close 23 May.

footer.tagline: Gen SEA Summit 2026 — Building amidst chaos.
